definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: I need some water. / I need some apples.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: I need some water. / I need some apples.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used for an unspecified amount in affirmative sentences.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used for an unspecified amount in affirmative sentences.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Do you have any money?]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: Used for an unspecified amount in questions.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used for an unspecified amount in questions.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: I don't have any money.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: Used for a zero amount in negative sentences.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used for a zero amount in negative sentences.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: I have no money.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: Used to indicate zero amount (stronger than 'not any').
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used to indicate zero amount (stronger than 'not any').]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: All the food is ready.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: Used to refer to the whole quantity or extent of something.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used to refer to the whole quantity or extent of something.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: I don't have much time.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: Used for a large amount of an uncountable noun (mainly in questions/negatives).
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used for a large amount of an uncountable noun (mainly in questions/negatives).]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: I have many friends.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: Used for a large number of a countable noun.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used for a large number of a countable noun.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: I have a lot of friends. / I have a lot of time.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: I have a lot of friends. / I have a lot of time.
definitions.usage_context_en: A large amount or number (used with both countable and uncountable nouns).
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: A large amount or number (used with both countable and uncountable nouns).]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: He has few friends.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: Used for a small number of a countable noun (implies a negative quantity, 'not enough').
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used for a small number of a countable noun (implies a negative quantity, 'not enough').]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: He has little time.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: Used for a small amount of an uncountable noun (implies a negative quantity, 'not enough').
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used for a small amount of an uncountable noun (implies a negative quantity, 'not enough').]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: I don't have enough money.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: As much or as many as is necessary or required.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: As much or as many as is necessary or required.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: I need more time.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: A greater or additional amount or degree.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: A greater or additional amount or degree.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: I have less money than you.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: I have less money than you.
definitions.usage_context_en: A smaller amount of (used with uncountable nouns).
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: A smaller amount of (used with uncountable nouns).]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Can I have another cookie?]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: Used for one more, additional, singular countable noun.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used for one more, additional, singular countable noun.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Let's meet another day.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: Used for a different singular countable noun.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used for a different singular countable noun.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Do you have other options?]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_context_en: Used for different or additional plural or uncountable nouns.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used for different or additional plural or uncountable nouns.]

definitions.usage_example: [TRADUCIR AL FR: The other key is on the table.]
definitions.pronunciation_guide_es: [PRONUNCIACIÓN EN FR PARA HABLANTES DE EN]
definitions.usage_example_es: The other key is on the table.
definitions.usage_context_en: Used with 'the' to refer to the second of two specific things.
definitions.usage_context_es: [TRADUCIR AL FR: Used with 'the' to refer to the second of two specific things.]
